faqs.question: Can I use AI to handle creating clickable-looking thumbnails without advanced design software without losing the core detail?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you choose the tool that matches the input type and then review the output yourself. Fotor is the best starting point, Freepik AI is the strongest second choice, and Midjourney is the safer fallback when you need a different balance of speed and control.

faqs.answer: Freepik AI is usually the better choice when source checking matters, while Fotor is stronger when the job starts with a known set of files or notes.

faqs.answer: Fotor is better when you want the most direct match for the main input; Freepik AI is better when the workflow changes enough to justify a different tool.
